---
title: ""
format:
  pdf:
    toc: false
    pdf-engine: pdflatex
    geometry:
      - margin=0.35in
      - landscape
    fontsize: 7pt
    documentclass: article
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{fvextra}
        \DefineVerbatimEnvironment{Highlighting}{Verbatim}{breaklines,commandchars=\\\{\}}
        \usepackage{xurl}
        \usepackage{enumitem}
        \setlist{nosep,leftmargin=*}
        \usepackage{longtable}
        \usepackage{booktabs}
        \usepackage{multicol}
        \usepackage{titlesec}
        \usepackage{colortbl}
        \usepackage{xcolor}
        \usepackage{array}
        \usepackage{tabularx}
        \definecolor{hdrblack}{HTML}{2D2D2D}
        \definecolor{hdrtext}{HTML}{FFFFFF}
        \definecolor{accentblue}{HTML}{1A5276}
        \titleformat{\section}{\normalfont\normalsize\bfseries\color{accentblue}}{}{0em}{}[\vspace{-0.5em}\rule{\columnwidth}{0.4pt}]
        \titleformat{\subsection}{\normalfont\small\bfseries}{}{0em}{}
        \titlespacing*{\section}{0pt}{4pt}{1pt}
        \titlespacing*{\subsection}{0pt}{3pt}{0pt}
        \pagestyle{empty}
        \setlength{\parindent}{0pt}
        \setlength{\parskip}{0.5pt}
        \setlength{\columnsep}{10pt}
        \newcommand{\cheatheader}[1]{\noindent\colorbox{hdrblack}{\parbox{\dimexpr\columnwidth-2\fboxsep}{\strut\textcolor{hdrtext}{\textbf{#1}}\strut}}\vspace{1pt}}
        \renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.1}
        \newcolumntype{C}{>{\ttfamily\raggedright\arraybackslash\footnotesize}p{0.52\columnwidth}}
        \newcolumntype{D}{>{\raggedright\arraybackslash\footnotesize}p{0.44\columnwidth}}
---

\begin{multicols}{2}

\noindent{\Large\bfseries\color{accentblue} System Administration Reference Cheatsheet}\\[1pt]
{\footnotesize Modern Linux (systemd) \textbar\ Legacy Linux (SysVinit) \textbar\ Windows --- Service, Package, File, and Backup Management}

\vspace{4pt}

\cheatheader{SERVICE MANAGEMENT}

\subsection*{Modern Linux (systemd)}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
systemctl start NAME & Start a service \\
systemctl stop NAME & Stop a service \\
systemctl restart NAME & Restart a service \\
systemctl reload NAME & Reload config (no restart) \\
systemctl enable NAME & Start at boot \\
systemctl disable NAME & Do not start at boot \\
systemctl status NAME & Status and recent logs \\
systemctl is-active NAME & Check if running \\
systemctl is-enabled NAME & Check if boot-enabled \\
systemctl list-units {-}{-}type=service & All loaded services \\
systemctl list-unit-files & All installed services \\
systemctl daemon-reload & Reload after unit file edits \\
systemctl mask NAME & Prevent starting entirely \\
journalctl -u NAME -f & Follow logs for service \\
\end{tabular}

\subsection*{Legacy Linux (SysVinit)}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
service NAME start|stop|restart & Control a service \\
service NAME status & Show status \\
chkconfig NAME on & Enable at boot (RHEL 5--6) \\
chkconfig NAME off & Disable at boot \\
chkconfig {-}{-}list & List all services/runlevels \\
update-rc.d NAME defaults & Enable at boot (Debian 6--7) \\
update-rc.d NAME remove & Disable at boot \\
/etc/init.d/NAME start & Direct init script control \\
\end{tabular}

\subsection*{Windows}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
sc query NAME & Show service status \\
sc start NAME / sc stop NAME & Start or stop \\
sc config NAME start= auto & Set to auto-start \\
sc config NAME start= disabled & Disable a service \\
sc qc NAME & Show config (binary path) \\
net start / net stop NAME & Start or stop \\
net start & List running services \\
Get-Service NAME & PS: service status \\
Restart-Service NAME & PS: restart \\
Set-Service NAME -StartupType Auto & PS: change startup type \\
sc query type= service state= all & List every service \\
\end{tabular}

\vspace{2pt}
\cheatheader{CREATING / EDITING SERVICES}

\subsection*{systemd unit file: /etc/systemd/system/NAME.service}

{\ttfamily\footnotesize
[Unit]\\
Description=My Custom Service\\
After=network.target\\[2pt]
[Service]\\
Type=simple\\
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/myapp\\
Restart=on-failure\\
User=appuser\\
WorkingDirectory=/opt/myapp\\[2pt]
[Install]\\
WantedBy=multi-user.target
}

Then: {\ttfamily\footnotesize systemctl daemon-reload \&\& systemctl enable {-}{-}now NAME}

\subsection*{SysVinit}

Create {\ttfamily /etc/init.d/NAME} (executable shell script with LSB header). Functions: {\ttfamily start()}, {\ttfamily stop()}, {\ttfamily restart()}, {\ttfamily status()}. Then {\ttfamily chmod +x} and {\ttfamily chkconfig {-}{-}add NAME} or {\ttfamily update-rc.d NAME defaults}.

\subsection*{Windows}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
sc create NAME binPath= "C:\textbackslash path\textbackslash app.exe" & Create a service \\
sc delete NAME & Delete a service \\
sc config NAME binPath= "..." & Change binary path \\
sc failure NAME reset= 86400 actions= restart/60000 & Auto-restart on fail \\
New-Service -Name NAME -BinaryPathName "path" & PS: create service \\
\end{tabular}

\vspace{2pt}
\cheatheader{PACKAGE MANAGEMENT}

\subsection*{Debian / Ubuntu (apt)}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
apt update & Refresh package index \\
apt install PKG & Install a package \\
apt remove PKG & Remove (keep config) \\
apt purge PKG & Remove + delete config \\
apt upgrade & Upgrade all packages \\
apt search TERM & Search for a package \\
apt list {-}{-}installed & List installed packages \\
dpkg -l & List all installed (detailed) \\
dpkg -L PKG & Files owned by package \\
dpkg -S /path/to/file & What package owns this file? \\
\end{tabular}

\subsection*{RHEL / CentOS / Fedora (dnf/yum)}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
dnf install PKG & Install (or yum on RHEL 5--7) \\
dnf remove PKG & Remove a package \\
dnf update & Update all packages \\
dnf search TERM & Search for a package \\
dnf list installed & List installed packages \\
rpm -qa & List all installed (rpm level) \\
rpm -ql PKG & Files owned by package \\
rpm -qf /path/to/file & What package owns this file? \\
\end{tabular}

\subsection*{Other Distros}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
zypper install PKG / zypper update & openSUSE / SLES \\
pacman -S PKG / pacman -Syu & Arch Linux \\
apk add PKG / apk upgrade & Alpine Linux \\
\end{tabular}

\subsection*{Windows}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
winget install PKG & Install from winget \\
winget search TERM & Search packages \\
winget upgrade {-}{-}all & Update all \\
winget list & List installed software \\
choco install PKG & Chocolatey: install \\
choco upgrade all & Chocolatey: update all \\
Get-WindowsFeature & List server roles/features \\
Install-WindowsFeature NAME & Install a role \\
DISM /Online /Enable-Feature /FeatureName:NAME & Enable Windows feature \\
\end{tabular}

\vspace{2pt}
\cheatheader{FINDING FILES: DATABASES}

\subsection*{Linux -- MySQL / MariaDB}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
find / -name "*.frm" 2>/dev/null & Table definition files \\
find / -name "ibdata*" 2>/dev/null & InnoDB data files \\
find / -name "my.cnf" -o -name "my.ini" & Config files \\
mysql -e "SHOW VARIABLES LIKE 'datadir';" & Data dir from MySQL \\
ss -tlnp | grep 3306 & Is MySQL listening? \\
\end{tabular}

Default configs: {\ttfamily /etc/mysql/my.cnf} (Debian), {\ttfamily /etc/my.cnf} (RHEL)

\subsection*{Linux -- PostgreSQL}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
find / -name "postgresql.conf" 2>/dev/null & Config file \\
find / -name "pg\_hba.conf" 2>/dev/null & Auth config \\
su - postgres -c "psql -c 'SHOW data\_directory;'" & Data directory \\
ss -tlnp | grep 5432 & Is Postgres listening? \\
\end{tabular}

\subsection*{Linux -- SQLite}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
find / \textbackslash( -name "*.db" -o -name "*.sqlite" -o -name "*.sqlite3" \textbackslash) 2>/dev/null & All SQLite databases \\
\end{tabular}

\subsection*{Windows -- SQL Server}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
Get-Service MSSQL* & Find SQL Server services \\
sqlcmd -S . -Q "SELECT name, physical\_name FROM sys.master\_files" & All DB file locations \\
dir /s *.mdf & Find data files \\
dir /s *.ldf & Find log files \\
\end{tabular}

\subsection*{Windows -- MySQL}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
sc query MySQL* & Find MySQL services \\
dir /s my.ini & Find config file \\
\end{tabular}

\vspace{2pt}
\cheatheader{FINDING FILES: WEB SERVERS}

\subsection*{Linux -- Apache}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
apachectl -S \textnormal{(or} httpd -S\textnormal{)} & Virtual hosts and doc roots \\
apache2ctl -M & Loaded modules \\
grep -r "DocumentRoot" /etc/apache2/ & Doc roots (Debian) \\
grep -r "DocumentRoot" /etc/httpd/ & Doc roots (RHEL) \\
find /etc -name "*.conf" -path "*/apache*" & All Apache configs \\
\end{tabular}

\subsection*{Linux -- Nginx}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
nginx -T & Dump full parsed config \\
grep -r "root " /etc/nginx/ & All document roots \\
grep -r "server\_name" /etc/nginx/ & All server names \\
\end{tabular}

\subsection*{Linux -- Generic}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
ss -tlnp | grep -E ':80 |:443 |:8080 ' & What listens on web ports? \\
find /var/www -type f -name "*.php" & PHP files in web root \\
\end{tabular}

\subsection*{Windows -- IIS}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
appcmd list site & List all IIS sites \\
appcmd list vdir & Virtual directories \\
Get-IISSite & PS: list sites \\
Get-WebBinding & PS: all bindings \\
\end{tabular}

Default root: {\ttfamily C:\textbackslash inetpub\textbackslash wwwroot}\\
Config: {\ttfamily C:\textbackslash Windows\textbackslash System32\textbackslash inetsrv\textbackslash config\textbackslash applicationHost.config}\\
{\footnotesize appcmd lives at {\ttfamily \%windir\%\textbackslash system32\textbackslash inetsrv\textbackslash appcmd.exe}}

\vspace{2pt}
\cheatheader{FINDING FILES: OTHER SERVICES}

\subsection*{Linux}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
ss -tlnp & All listening TCP + process \\
ss -ulnp & All listening UDP + process \\
find /etc -name "named.conf*" & BIND DNS config \\
grep -r "zone " /etc/named* /etc/bind/* & DNS zone files \\
postconf -n & Postfix non-default settings \\
cat /etc/vsftpd.conf & vsftpd FTP config \\
testparm -s & Effective Samba config \\
smbstatus & Samba connections/shares \\
find /etc -name "slapd*" & OpenLDAP config \\
\end{tabular}

\subsection*{Windows}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
netstat -abno & All ports + PIDs + names \\
Get-NetTCPConnection -State Listen & PS: listening ports \\
dnscmd /enumzones & List DNS zones \\
Get-DnsServerZone & PS: list DNS zones \\
dcdiag /v & DC diagnostics \\
repadmin /replsummary & Replication health \\
nltest /dclist:DOMAIN & List all DCs \\
netsh dhcp server show scope & DHCP scopes \\
net share & List file shares \\
Get-SmbShare & PS: shares \\
\end{tabular}

\vspace{2pt}
\cheatheader{CREATING BACKUPS}

\subsection*{Linux -- Files and Directories}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
tar czf /backup/etc.tar.gz /etc & Compress /etc \\
tar czf /backup/home.tar.gz /home & Compress /home \\
tar czf /backup/www.tar.gz /var/www & Compress web roots \\
tar xzf /backup/etc.tar.gz -C /restore & Extract a backup \\
rsync -av /src/ /dst/ & Incremental sync \\
rsync -av -e ssh /src/ user@host:/dst/ & Remote sync via SSH \\
cp -a /etc /backup/etc-\$(date +\%F) & Copy with timestamp \\
\end{tabular}

\subsection*{Linux -- Database Backups}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
mysqldump -u root -p {-}{-}all-databases > all.sql & Dump all MySQL DBs \\
mysqldump -u root -p DBNAME > db.sql & Dump one MySQL DB \\
mysql -u root -p DBNAME < db.sql & Restore MySQL \\
pg\_dumpall -U postgres > all.sql & Dump all PostgreSQL \\
pg\_dump -U postgres DBNAME > db.sql & Dump one PostgreSQL DB \\
psql -U postgres DBNAME < db.sql & Restore PostgreSQL \\
sqlite3 /path/db .dump > bak.sql & Dump SQLite \\
\end{tabular}

\subsection*{Linux -- Quick Backup Script}

{\ttfamily\footnotesize
\#!/bin/bash\\
D=\$(date +\%F-\%H\%M); B=/backup/\$D; mkdir -p \$B\\
tar czf \$B/etc.tar.gz /etc\\
tar czf \$B/www.tar.gz /var/www /srv 2>/dev/null\\
mysqldump -u root {-}{-}all-databases | gzip > \$B/mysql.sql.gz\\
cp /etc/fstab /etc/passwd /etc/shadow /etc/group \$B/\\
crontab -l > \$B/root-crontab.txt 2>/dev/null\\
systemctl list-unit-files > \$B/services.txt\\
echo "Backup complete: \$B"
}

\subsection*{Windows -- System and Files}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
wbadmin start systemstatebackup -backupTarget:E: & System State backup \\
wbadmin start backup -backupTarget:E: -include:C: & Full volume backup \\
robocopy C:\textbackslash src E:\textbackslash dst /MIR /ZB /R:1 /W:1 & Mirror copy \\
\end{tabular}

\subsection*{Windows -- Registry, Policy, Firewall}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
reg export HKLM\textbackslash SOFTWARE out.reg & Export registry key \\
reg import out.reg & Import registry key \\
secedit /export /cfg sec.cfg & Export security policy \\
netsh advfirewall export fw.wfw & Export firewall rules \\
netsh advfirewall import fw.wfw & Import firewall rules \\
\end{tabular}

\subsection*{Windows -- AD and Database}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
ntdsutil "ac i ntds" "ifm" "create full C:\textbackslash bak" q q & AD IFM backup \\
Backup-GPO -All -Path C:\textbackslash GPOBackup & Backup all GPOs \\
sqlcmd -Q "BACKUP DATABASE [db] TO DISK='C:\textbackslash b.bak'" & SQL Server backup \\
\end{tabular}

\vspace{2pt}
\cheatheader{QUICK TRIAGE: WHAT CHANGED?}

\subsection*{Linux}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
find / -mmin -60 -type f 2>/dev/null & Files modified in last hour \\
find / -mtime -1 -type f 2>/dev/null & Files modified in last 24h \\
last -20 & Last 20 logins \\
lastb -20 & Last 20 failed logins \\
history & Command history (current user) \\
cat /home/*/.bash\_history & All users' histories \\
ausearch -ts recent & Recent audit events \\
debsums -c & Changed package files (Debian) \\
rpm -Va & Changed package files (RHEL) \\
\end{tabular}

\subsection*{Windows}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
Get-ChildItem C:\textbackslash\ -Recurse | Where \{\$\_.LastWriteTime -gt (Get-Date).AddHours(-1)\} & Files modified in last hour \\
wevtutil qe Security /c:20 /f:text & Last 20 security events \\
Get-EventLog Security -Newest 50 & Recent security events \\
query user & Who is logged in? \\
qwinsta & All sessions (incl.\ RDP) \\
sfc /verifyonly & System file integrity \\
\end{tabular}

\vspace{2pt}
\cheatheader{USER AND PERMISSION MANAGEMENT}

\subsection*{Linux}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
useradd -m USERNAME & Create user with home dir \\
userdel -r USERNAME & Delete user + home dir \\
passwd USERNAME & Change password \\
usermod -aG GROUP USER & Add user to group \\
gpasswd -d USER GROUP & Remove from group \\
chown USER:GROUP FILE & Change ownership \\
chmod 750 FILE & Set permissions (rwxr-x---) \\
grep "sudo\textbackslash|wheel" /etc/group & Who has sudo? \\
visudo & Edit sudoers safely \\
passwd -l USERNAME & Lock account \\
passwd -u USERNAME & Unlock account \\
chage -l USERNAME & Password expiry info \\
\end{tabular}

\subsection*{Windows}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
net user USERNAME PASS /add & Create local user \\
net user USERNAME /del & Delete user \\
net localgroup GROUP USER /add & Add to group \\
net localgroup GROUP USER /del & Remove from group \\
net localgroup Administrators & List local admins \\
icacls FILE & View permissions \\
icacls FILE /grant USER:F & Grant full control \\
takeown /F FILE /R & Take ownership \\
net user USERNAME /active:no & Disable account \\
\end{tabular}

\subsection*{Windows -- Active Directory}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
New-ADUser -Name "user" -Enabled \$true & Create AD user \\
Add-ADGroupMember "Group" "User" & Add to AD group \\
Disable-ADAccount -Identity USER & Disable AD account \\
Search-ADAccount -LockedOut & Find locked accounts \\
Unlock-ADAccount -Identity USER & Unlock account \\
\end{tabular}

\vspace{2pt}
\cheatheader{FIREWALL QUICK REFERENCE}

\subsection*{Linux -- iptables}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
iptables -L -n -v & List all rules \\
iptables -A INPUT -p tcp {-}{-}dport 22 -j ACCEPT & Allow SSH \\
iptables -A INPUT -s IP -j DROP & Block an IP \\
iptables -P INPUT DROP & Default deny inbound \\
iptables-save > /etc/iptables.rules & Save rules \\
iptables-restore < /etc/iptables.rules & Restore rules \\
\end{tabular}

\subsection*{Linux -- firewalld (RHEL 7+)}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
firewall-cmd {-}{-}list-all & Show current rules \\
firewall-cmd {-}{-}add-port=80/tcp {-}{-}permanent & Open port \\
firewall-cmd {-}{-}reload & Apply changes \\
\end{tabular}

\subsection*{Windows}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
netsh advfirewall show allprofiles & Firewall status \\
netsh advfirewall set allprofiles state on & Enable firewall \\
netsh advfirewall firewall add rule name="Allow SSH" dir=in action=allow protocol=TCP localport=22 & Allow a port inbound \\
netsh advfirewall firewall add rule name="Block" dir=in action=block remoteip=IP & Block an IP \\
netsh advfirewall firewall show rule name=all & List all rules \\
\end{tabular}

\vspace{2pt}
\cheatheader{COMMON CONFIG FILE LOCATIONS}

\subsection*{Linux}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
/etc/ssh/sshd\_config & SSH server config \\
/etc/passwd, /etc/shadow & User accounts / passwords \\
/etc/group, /etc/gshadow & Groups \\
/etc/sudoers, /etc/sudoers.d/ & Sudo rules \\
/etc/fstab & Filesystem mounts \\
/etc/hosts, /etc/resolv.conf & DNS / hostname resolution \\
/etc/crontab, /var/spool/cron/ & Scheduled tasks \\
/etc/rsyslog.conf & Syslog config \\
/var/log/auth.log \textnormal{(Deb)} & Authentication log \\
/var/log/secure \textnormal{(RHEL)} & Authentication log \\
/var/log/syslog \textnormal{(Deb)} & System log \\
/var/log/messages \textnormal{(RHEL)} & System log \\
\end{tabular}

\subsection*{Windows}

\begin{tabular}{@{}CD@{}}
C:\textbackslash Windows\textbackslash System32\textbackslash drivers\textbackslash etc\textbackslash hosts & Hosts file \\
C:\textbackslash Windows\textbackslash System32\textbackslash config\textbackslash & SAM, SECURITY, SYSTEM \\
C:\textbackslash Windows\textbackslash System32\textbackslash winevt\textbackslash Logs\textbackslash & Event log files \\
C:\textbackslash inetpub\textbackslash wwwroot\textbackslash & IIS default web root \\
C:\textbackslash ProgramData\textbackslash & App config/data (hidden) \\
\%APPDATA\% & Per-user app config \\
\end{tabular}

\end{multicols}
